question.text: An investor in the 30% tax bracket earned a total return of Rs. 1,00,000 from a debt mutual fund purchased in September 2024 and redeemed after 6 months. His effective post-tax return (including cess) as a percentage of the gain is:

answer.text: 68.8% of the gain (31.2% effective rate including cess)
87% of the gain (13% effective rate with indexation)

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Debt MF purchased after April 2023: all gains taxed at slab rate. For 30% bracket investor: Tax rate = 30% + 4% cess on tax = 30% + 1.2% = 31.2% effective rate. Tax on Rs. 1,00,000 = Rs. 31,200. Post-tax return = Rs. 1,00,000 - Rs. 31,200 = Rs. 68,800 = 68.8% of the gain retained.
**The Trap:** Option A uses 15% STCG rate (applicable to equity, not debt). Option B uses 20% flat rate. Option D assumes indexation is available (removed for post-April 2023 debt fund purchases).
**Cross-Exam Context:** Understanding effective tax rates (including cess) is important for comparing MF returns with other products. A 30% bracket investor keeps only 68.8% of debt MF gains, making it comparable to FD taxation.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For high-tax-bracket clients, post-2023 debt MFs have the same tax treatment as FDs. Position them on liquidity, no TDS (below threshold), and professional management rather than tax efficiency.
